evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Keep data trustworthy by adding tests and validation practices
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signal (data trust, testing, validation) balances against velocity-flavoured customer-facing culture language ("craft killer demos"), giving parity.
evidence.domain_risk: helping data teams build a trusted, supervised self-service analytics experience
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Internal BI tooling and presales demo work carry limited, recoverable consequences with no financial reporting or regulatory language present.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build best-practice data models using dbt with dimensional modeling approaches
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Established best-practice dbt/dimensional modeling conventions and an existing internal data pipeline indicate a functioning, moderately mature internal data practice.
evidence.jd_authorship: Build with coding agents & dogfood using AI tools like Development Copilot and Claude Code/Cursor to develop models on the analytics-as-code layer
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Highly specific technical detail (AI coding agents, analytics-as-code layer, schema reviews, RFPs) gives a concrete picture of the dual internal/presales role.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Support RFPs & technical Q&A paired with Sales on deep technical questions
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role splits roughly equally between internal data/analytics engineering work and commercial presales/sales-partnership work, giving mixed orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Partner with the sales reps joining solutioning calls to own technical substance
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Owning technical substance in solutioning calls is ownership-leaning, but most responsibilities are framed as "contribute to", "support", and "partner with", giving mixed autonomy.
evidence.ai_role: Build with coding agents & dogfood using AI tools like Development Copilot and Claude Code/Cursor to develop models
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is explicitly expected to use AI coding tools (Development Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor) to accelerate their own model-building work, the clearest ai_user signal.
evidence.testing_framing: Keep data trustworthy by adding tests and validation practices
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Adding tests and validation practices is named as an owned responsibility with an explicit action verb ("adding"), not a passive tool mention.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Keep data trustworthy by adding tests and validation practices
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data trust is a stated concern, but it is framed as internal pipeline reliability rather than compliance or regulatory risk.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Contribute to our internal data pipeline to maintain ingestion, transformation, and reporting for stakeholders
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role mixes maintaining existing internal data pipelines with building new best-practice data models and customer-facing solutions.
evidence.language_gate: Good communication in English (written and spoken)
evidence.loss_aversion: Keep data trustworthy by adding tests and validation practices — reflects concern for internal data reliability rather than compliance or regulatory risk.
